title: rhinanthus alectorolophus subsp. buccalis
intro: Rhinanthus alectorolophus subsp. buccalis, also known as the yellow rattle or yellow bird's tongue, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae, and is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of other plants. Traditionally, Rhinanthus alectorolophus subsp. buccalis has been used for its medicinal properties, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive problems like diarrhea and dyspepsia. Rhinanthus alectorolophus subsp. buccalis can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various ailments due to its purported medicinal properties.
